concept: 9th-gen Toreador artist frozen in a 1960s hippie aesthetic who values community over control
appearance: A walking relic of the late 1960s counterculture. Lila dresses exactly as she did at the height of her mortal life: flowing skirts, bell-bottoms, fringe vests, embroidered blouses, headbands, beads, and round tinted glasses.

appearance_detailed.short_summary: Radiant Toreador hippie with timeless warmth and unmistakable artistic presence.
appearance_detailed.detailed_description: Lila looks like she stepped out of a psychedelic concert poster and never bothered to leave. Her clothes are layered, colorful, and unapologetically anachronistic—sun-faded fabrics, handmade jewelry, soft leather sandals, and scarves that smell faintly of incense. Her open expressions and intense focus around art make her emotions easy to read to anyone paying attention.

biography: Embraced at the tail end of the 1960s, Lila was a gifted poster artist and muralist whose work blended color, symbolism, and emotion in ways her sire found irresistible. Immortality did not temper her ideals—it fossilized them. Decades later, she still believes art should be shared, people should not be owned, and undeath does not excuse cruelty. She refuses to keep ghouls, finding the blood bond morally repugnant, and instead surrounds herself with other Kindred to stave off loneliness. Joining the coterie was less a political move than an emotional one: she likes having people around, and she believes community is the closest thing vampires have to salvation.
notes: Toreador clan weakness applies normally. Soft-Skinned increases frequency and severity of emotional and aesthetic Entrancement.
equipment: Art supplies, sketchbooks, paints, incense, portable record player, vinyl albums, poster tubes, notebooks filled with designs and poetry.

domain_and_haven.location_notes: Prefers shared or communal living spaces over isolated havens.
domain_and_haven.false_public_assumptions: Harmless eccentric artist who never grew out of the sixties.

personality.tagline: Art is connection. Control is a lie.
personality.narrative: Lila is warm, expressive, and deeply emotionally porous. Beauty, cruelty, and suffering all hit her harder than she expects, and powerful art can overwhelm her to the point of forgetting everything else.

backgroundDetails.Resources: Income from art commissions and patronage.

merits_flaws.name: Artistic Genius
merits_flaws.description: Her art is exceptional, emotionally powerful, and immediately recognizable.

merits_flaws.name: Soft-Skinned
merits_flaws.description: Exceptionally sensitive to emotion and beauty; emotional stimuli and art more easily overwhelm her and worsen Toreador Entrancement.

merits_flaws.name: Ennui
merits_flaws.description: Periodic bouts of existential boredom and disconnection from the world.
